All programs in development. Addresses affordable housing shortage and senior aging-in-place needs across Greater Tampa Bay.

sub_programs.name: Affordable Home Development
sub_programs.description: Helps low-income families access safe, affordable housing through new construction, rehabilitation, homebuyer education, and down payment assistance.

sub_programs.name: Elderly Housing Support
sub_programs.description: Supports older adults aging in place via home modifications, senior-focused housing development, resource navigation, and isolation prevention.

sub_programs.name: Housing Stability Assistance
sub_programs.description: Emergency rental assistance, eviction prevention, housing case management, and rapid rehousing.

strategy.vision: Become the premier Two-Generation family-strengthening nonprofit in Tampa Bay — and a model for what a lean, community-rooted 501(c)(3) can accomplish.
strategy.geographic_focus: Tampa Bay / Hillsborough County primary. International aspirations long-term — to be confirmed as the organization matures.
strategy.model: Two-Generation (Two-Gen): we serve children and their parents simultaneously across 5 interconnected program areas. This is our competitive differentiator with funders.
strategy.why_it_matters_for_board: Every program we run, every grant we apply for, every dollar we document — all of it builds a 3-year track record. Funders evaluate the board as credibility. Board members are not just governance — you are the foundation that unlocks funding.

phases.goal: Build the legal and digital foundation.
priorities: Incorporated as Florida nonprofit + 501(c)(3) approved
EIN obtained + Suncoast business checking account opened
Website launched — icaregives.org (37 pages, 16 programs, Zeffy donations)
6 community events delivered — food bank, back-to-school, Thanksliving, MedShare
Phase 1 complete

phases.goal: Establish credibility. Get the compliance house in order so funders trust us.
priorities: Adopt bylaws + formally seat 2026 board (April 11)
File 990-N (e-Postcard) — builds public record grantors rely on
Create GuideStar/Candid profile — required by most foundations
Register SAM.gov — required for ANY federal grant
Land first external grants: Publix (April 30), Bank of America (June 29)
Year 1 funding target: $75K–$125K

phases.goal: Expand programming, add paid staff, pursue first federal grant.
priorities: First federal grant application (requires 2 years 990 history + SAM.gov)
Expand food pantry to documented monthly operations with attendance tracking
Launch career development and health programs with measurable outcomes
Build relationship with Community Foundation Tampa Bay (Dec 1 cycle)
Year 2 funding target: $200K–$300K

phases.goal: Become a recognized lead Two-Gen provider in Hillsborough County.
priorities: Eligible for larger foundation grants requiring 3 years of 990 history
Pursue HHS/ACF HMRF (up to $6.25M / 5-year federal award) — confirm eligibility requirements
Paid program staff in core areas
Long-term funding target: $500K+

strategy.program_priority_rationale: Food Security (#1) has the most documented 2025 activity and strongest immediate grant match. Family Strengthening (#2) unlocks the largest long-term federal funding. Education & Youth (#3), Health & Wellness (#4), and Career Development (#5) build the Two-Gen case across all applications.
what_board_members_control: Vote on all major decisions (4 of 5 required for bylaws, compensation, dissolution)
Support annual IRS filing (Form 990-N) — Treasurer reviews financials, President certifies
Authorize the President as AOR on every grant application
Attend and document attendance — quorum (3 of 5) required for valid votes
Sign annual Conflict of Interest disclosures — required by most grantors
